title: Dichanthelium consanguineum
taxonomy: Dichanthelium consanguineum is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It belongs to the genus Dichanthelium, which consists of several species of grasses found in North America. The genus Dichanthelium is closely related to the genera Gymnostachys and Puccinellia, and is part of the subfamily Pooideae. The classification of Dichanthelium consanguineum is based on its morphological characteristics and molecular analysis. Further research is needed to fully understand the relationships within the genus Dichanthelium.

intro: Dichanthelium consanguineum, also known as Tufted Hairgrass, is a perennial grass species native to North America. The plant has been used in traditional herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, sore throats, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Dichanthelium consanguineum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and salves, taking advantage of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
